how to create a purchase order po.answer:
# How to Create a Purchase Order (PO)

A **Purchase Order** is a legally binding document between a supplier and a buyer that details items to be purchased at a specific price, delivery date, and payment terms. Follow these steps to create a PO in Ginesys Web.

## Prerequisites

Before creating a PO, ensure:
- Your user role has access to the **Add** app operation in **Purchase Order** feature (Procurement - Operation - Order) in **Admin > Security > Roles**
- **Document Numbering Scheme** is created for Purchase Orders
- Master data exists: **Items**, **Vendors**, **Agents**, etc.

## Step-by-Step Guide

### 1. Navigate to Purchase Order Module
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Procurement > Operations > Order > Purchase Order**
- The **Purchase Order** module opens with a list of previously created orders

### 2. Click Add Button
- Click the **Add** button
- The **Purchase Order** window opens

### 3. Fill General Section

- **Owner Site** (Mandatory) — Select the site for which you're creating the PO
- **Order No.** (Mandatory) — Select a **Document Numbering Scheme** from the dropdown
- **Vendor Name** (Mandatory) — Click to open the Vendor Pop-Up and select a vendor
  - **Purchase Type**, **Term**, **Currency**, **Exchange Rate**, and **Trade Group** auto-populate based on vendor selection
- **Date** (Mandatory) — System date is auto-populated; you can change it to a past date (not future)
- **Entry Mode** — Select either **Item** or **Set** (if selecting Set, you'll choose Item Sets instead of individual items)
- Additional optional fields: **Transporter**, **Agent**, **Commission Rate**, **Merchandiser Name**

### 4. Add Items to Purchase Order

Choose one of these methods to add items:

#### Search Item
- Click **Search Item**
- Enter search criteria and click **Search**
- Select items from results
- Items populate with **Barcode**, **Item Description**, **Rate**, **Qty.**, **Amount**

#### Scan Item
- Click **Scan Item** and scan barcodes directly

#### Import Barcode
- Use .csv or .txt files to populate items

#### Import Excel
- Use Excel files to import multiple items

#### Item Combination
- Create multiple items with different category combinations (e.g., 5 sizes × 6 colors = 30 items automatically)

#### Copy Order
- Copy items from an existing order

### 5. Edit Items (If Needed)
- Click the **dropdown arrow** on the left of any item to access:
  - **Delete Record** — Remove the item
  - **Item Charge** — View/modify charges applied to that item
  - **View Properties** — Check item details (read-only)

### 6. Calculate Charges
- Click **Calculate Charge** button
- Charges auto-calculate on save if you skip this step
- Click on any charge to modify its factors (if enabled in your user profile)
- Final **Net Amount** displays after charge application

### 7. Add Remarks & Terms (Optional)
- Go to **Others** section
- Enter **Remarks** and **Terms** as needed

### 8. Save Purchase Order
- Click **Save** button
- A dialog appears: *"Order will be authorized after save. Do you want to Authorize?"*
  - Click **Yes** to authorize and save
  - Click **No** to save without authorization
- Confirmation message: *"Document <Purchase Order No.> saved successfully"*

---

## Additional Tips

🔧 **Toolbar Icons** (Top Right):
- **Expand All** — Expand all collapsed sections
- **Collapse All** — Collapse all expanded sections
- **Attachments** — Upload required documents
- **Clear Form** — Clear all entered data
- **Open Side Panel** — View Document Summary, Vendor Details, and Statistics

⚠️ **Important Notes**:
- If Entry Mode is **Set**, select Item Sets, not individual items
- You must select **Term Name** in General section to calculate charges on ad-hoc items
- You can only select **past dates**, not future dates

---

## Sources

how to create a goods receive challan gr.answer:
# How to Create a Goods Receive Challan (GRC)

A **Goods Receive Challan (GRC)** is a record used to confirm that delivered goods have been received and is often compared to a Purchase Order before payment through Invoicing is issued. GRC affects **Inventory** but has no impact on **Finance**.

---

## Prerequisites

Before creating a GRC, ensure:

- Your user role has access to the **Add** app operation in the **Receipt** feature (Procurement - Operation - Goods In/Out) in **Admin > Security > Roles**
- **Document Numbering Scheme** is created for GRC (and PI if auto-creating Purchase Invoice)
- Master data exists:
  - **Items** in Inventory Module
  - **Vendors** in Procurement Module
  - **Stores** with operations started
  - **Organization/Managed Sites** connected

- User Profile settings (Admin > Security > User Profile > Procurement tab):
  - ✅ **Allow selection of items from Order mode in Receipts** (for order items)
  - ✅ **Allow Adhoc item selection in Receipts** (for adhoc items)
  - ✅ **Allow Set item selection in Receipts** (for set items)
  - ✅ **Display Purchase rate and value for Receipts** (optional)
  - ✅ **Allow item price population from GRC** (optional)

---

## Step-by-Step Guide

### 1. Navigate to GRC Module

- Go to **Ginesys Web > Procurement > Operations > Goods In/Out > Receipt**
- The **Goods Receive Challan** module opens with a list of previously created GRCs
- Click **Add** button
- **Goods Receive Challan** window opens

---

### 2. Fill General Section

- **Owner Site** (Mandatory) — Select the site for which GRC is being created
  - Auto-populated with connected site; can be changed before saving
  - **Cannot be modified in Edit mode**

- **Receipt No.** (Mandatory) — Select a **Document Numbering Scheme** from dropdown

- **Vendor Name** (Mandatory) — Select from dropdown
  - **Type**, **Trade Group Name**, **Term Name**, **Order Currency**, **Exchange Rate** auto-populate

- **Date** (Mandatory) — System date auto-populated; can change to past date

- **Stock Point** (Mandatory) — Auto-populated based on vendor; can be modified

- **Exchange Rate** — Auto-populated from Currency Master; **can be modified**
  - Modification recalculates rates for **Order items only**, not Adhoc items
  - Confirmation alert: *"Modification of Exchange Rate will recalculate Rates of items selected against an order only. Please confirm."*

---

### 3. Fill Logistic Section (If Applicable)

- If logistics is applicable for the selected vendor, **Logistic** section enables
- **Select mandatory fields before adding items**
- New field: **Shipment Tracking Applicable** — for tracking order items

---

### 4. Add Items to GRC

#### **For Adhoc Items** (without Purchase Order):

Select one of these methods:

**Search Item**
- Click **Search Item**
- Enter search criteria
- Click **Search**
- Select items from results
- Click **Populate**
- If batch/serial managed item: **Select Batch/Serial Detail Window** opens
  - Choose batch/serial numbers with quantities
  - Click **Populate** to add to main grid

**Scan Item**
- Click **Scan Item**
- Scan item barcode(s)
- For batch/serial managed items: window opens for batch selection
- Quantity auto-increases for duplicate scans

**Create Item**
- Click **Create Item** to create new items on-the-fly
- Item saves directly to Item Master

**Import Barcode**
- Use .csv or .txt files to import items

**Import Excel**
- Use Excel file to import multiple items with details

**Copy Challan**
- Copy item details from existing challans
- Only receipts from current financial year, connected OU, and connected site display
- Selected items populate with **Barcode**, **Item Description**, **Rate**, **Order No.**, **Order Date**

#### **For Order Items** (against Purchase Order):

**Select Order Item**
- Click **Select Order Item**
- **Order Mode** window opens
- Choose one of two options:

**Option 1: Select an order and populate all pending items**
- Select **Purchase Order** from dropdown
- Click **Show Item** button
- Items from that order list with **Order No., Valid Upto, Tolerance, Pending Qty., Receive Qty.**
- Click **Receive all Pending Qty.** to receive all items at once, or select specific items
- Click **Populate**

**Option 2: Select an item and populate all pending orders**
- Scan item barcode in **Item Barcode** field
- All pending orders for that item display
- Select orders and quantities
- Click **Populate**

#### **For Set Items** (Order mode only):

**Select Set**
- Click **Select Set**
- Select **Set Order No.** (Item sets only available against orders)
- Select the **Set(s)** from list
- Set populates with **Set Code, Item Count, Pending Item Qty., Pending Item Set, Receive Qty., Receive Set**
- Click **Receive all Pending Sets** or select specific sets
- Click **Populate**

#### **For Alternate Items**:

**Alternate Order Item**
- Click **Alternate Order Item**
- Select **Purchase Order** and the ordered **Item**
- Select matching receive items through **Select Item, Scan Item, Create Item**
- Items display with quantity totals
- Click **Populate**

---

### 5. Manage Items

For each populated item, click the **dropdown arrow** on the left to access:

- **Delete Record** — Remove the item
- **Price Population** — Change item price from a specific date/site
- **Item Charge** — View/modify charges applied to item
- **View Properties** — Check item details (read-only; opens Item Master)
- **Edit Item** — Edit the item (saves directly to Item Master)

---

### 6. Calculate Charges

- Click **Calculate Charge** button
- Charges auto-calculate on save if skipped
- Click on any charge to view/modify factors (if enabled in user profile)
- Final **Net Amount** displays after charge application

⚠️ **Important**:
- For **Adhoc items**: **Term Name** must be selected in General section
- For **Order items**: Charges calculated per respective Purchase Order
- If no Term selected for Adhoc items, message appears: *"Term selection is required to calculate charges of items without Order. Please select Term to calculate charges."*

---

### 7. Fill Others Section

- Enter **Remarks** if needed

---

### 8. Save GRC

- Click **Save** button
- If Margin Rule violated: **Invalid Margin Rule Items** window opens
  - ✏️ Edit red-marked amounts or click **Populate Corrected Value**
  - Or click **Ignore and Save** (if enabled in user profile)
- Confirmation: *"Document <GRC No.> saved successfully"*

---

### 9. Auto-Generate Purchase Invoice (Optional)

- Message appears: *"Do you want to generate Purchase Invoice against this Receipt?"*
- **Only shows if**:
  - GRC is **Non-Consignment** type
  - **Outright mode** selected
  - Auto PI generation enabled in user profile
  - All conditions met in settings

- Click **Yes**:
  - **Purchase Invoice** window opens
  - **Vendor Name, Reference Site, Vendor Ledger, Trade Group** auto-populate
  - **System Date** in Invoice Date field
  - Enter **Document No., Document Date** (≥ system date), **Purchase Ledger**
  - Click **Generate**
  - Confirmation: *"Invoice No. <PI No.> successfully generated"*

- Click **No**: New GRC created without PI

---

## Types of GRC

| Type | Description | When Used |
|------|-------------|-----------|
| **GRC-Adhoc** | Created **without Purchase Order** | Emergency receipts or goods received without prior order |
| **GRC Against Order** | Created **against Purchase Order** | Normal goods receipt matching PO |
| **Mixed GRC** | Contains **both Adhoc and Order items** | Single receipt with items from multiple sources |

---

## Key Features

✨ **New in Latest Version**:
- **Batch/Serial columns** added (Batch/Serial No., Mfg. Date, Valid Upto) — view-only
- **Owner Site** field — can be changed before save
- **Mixed GRC support** — combine Adhoc + Order items in one receipt
- **Multiple PO support** — select items from different Purchase Orders in single GRC
- **Auto Charge Calculation** — charges calculate on save
- **Exchange Rate modification** — recalculates order item rates
- **Auto Release** — GRC for Organization Sites auto-releases
- **Auto PI Generation** — generates Purchase Invoice on save (if conditions met)

---

## Important Notes

⚠️ **Restrictions**:
- **Cannot modify Owner Site in Edit mode**
- **Cannot modify Currency if Order items selected**
- **Exchange Rate modification** only affects Order items, not Adhoc items
- **Batch/Serial numbers cannot be modified** — delete and re-add to change
- Quantity **not editable** for rows linked to orders

📋 **User Profile Requirements**:
- Each item selection mode requires separate profile permission
- Admin must assign appropriate profile settings
- Re-login required after profile changes

---

## Sources

📖 [Adding, Editing, Deleting & Printing GRC (Goods Receive Challan)](https://ginesysone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/PUB/pages/28863473)

📖 [GRC (Goods Receive Challan) FAQs](https://ginesysone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/PUB/pages/28865379)

📖 [How To: Create Goods Receive Challan against GRC Advice at POS Back Office](https://ginesysone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/GPOS/pages/29200659)

Thank you for your interest in Ginesys POS! 

Pricing for Ginesys solutions depends on several factors and is **customized based on your specific requirements**:

---

## 🔍 **Factors That Affect Pricing**

- **Number of POS terminals** (3 in your case)
- **Number of stores/locations**
- **Modules required** (POS, Inventory, GRC, GST, E-Way Bills, Reports, etc.)
- **Business scale** (Annual turnover, transaction volume)
- **Additional features** (Loyalty, Promotions, Multi-store sync, Cloud/On-Premise)
- **Support & maintenance packages**
- **Implementation & training services**

---

## 📞 **How to Get Your Custom Quote**

### **Contact Options**:

1. **Visit Ginesys Website** → [www.ginesys.com](https://www.ginesys.com)
   - Click **"Get a Demo"** or **"Request Pricing"**

2. **Call Ginesys Sales**
   - Dial your regional sales office

3. **Email Sales Team**
   - sales@ginesys.com

---

## ✅ **What to Share With Sales Team**

To get an accurate quote, have ready:

- ✓ Number of POS terminals (3)
- ✓ Number of stores/locations
- ✓ Business type (Retail, QSR, Apparel, etc.)
- ✓ Key modules needed (Inventory, GST, Reports, etc.)
- ✓ Expected monthly transaction volume
- ✓ Preference: Cloud or On-Premise setup
- ✓ Budget timeline

---

## 💡 **Why Custom Pricing?**

Ginesys POS is **highly flexible** and scalable — pricing reflects:
- Your exact business needs
- Volume discounts for larger deployments
- Bundled module discounts
- Training & support inclusions
- Long-term partnership benefits

---

## 🎯 **Next Steps**

**I recommend**: Contact our Sales Consultant for a **free consultation & demo** to see Ginesys POS in action for your business model.

**Ready to explore?** → Reach out to **sales@ginesys.com** or visit the website!

how to configure gst rates and gstin.answer:
# How to Configure GST Rates and GSTIN

GST (Goods and Services Tax) configuration in Ginesys involves two main components: **GSTIN (GST Identification Number)** setup and **GST Rate** management. Follow this comprehensive guide.

---

## Part 1: Defining the GSTIN Master

### Prerequisites

Before setting up GSTIN:
- Your user role must have the **Add** application operation enabled for **GST Identification Number (GSTIN)** feature in **Admin > Organization > Manage through Roles** (Web - Admin - Security)
- You must have applied for and obtained the **GSTIN from the Government of India** for each Organization Unit

### What is GSTIN?

**GSTIN** is a unique 15-digit identification number assigned by the Government of India to each industrial unit (Organization Unit in Ginesys) based on the State to which they belong.

#### GSTIN Format Breakdown:

| Digit Position | Details |
|---|---|
| **Digits 1-2** | State Code (e.g., "27" for Maharashtra, "10" for Bihar) |
| **Digits 3-12** | PAN Number of taxpayer (10 digits) |
| **Digit 13** | Registration number within state (1-9, then A-Z for up to 35 registrations) |
| **Digit 14** | Default value "Z" (currently unused) |
| **Digit 15** | Check digit (error detection code) |

### Step-by-Step: Add GSTIN

**1. Navigate to GSTIN Master**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Organization > Manage > GST Identification Number (GSTIN)**

**2. Click Add Button**
- The **Add GST Identification Number (GSTIN)** window opens

**3. Fill General Section** (All Mandatory)

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| **Organization Unit** | Select the Organization Unit for which GSTIN is being defined (unique for each entity) |
| **GSTIN No.** | Enter the exact 15-digit GSTIN received from Government of India |
| **Registration Date** | Date on which the Organization Unit was provided the GSTIN |
| **State** | Auto-populated based on first 2 digits of GSTIN (display field only) |
| **E-Invoice Applicable** | Select **YES** or **NO** from dropdown (default: NO) |

**4. Add PPOB (Optional)**
- **Principal Place of Business** — Main place of business in a state (used in reports only)

**5. Map GST Ledgers**

Create separate **Input and Output ledgers** for each GST type and tag them here:
- **Integrated GST (IGST)** — Input & Output
- **Central GST (CGST)** — Input & Output
- **State/Union Territory GST (SGST/UTGST)** — Input & Output
- **Compensation Cess** — Input & Output

**Reference Module:** Admin > Finance > Ledgers

**6. Save GSTIN**
- Click **Save** button
- GSTIN is now configured for the Organization Unit

---

### Edit GSTIN (If Needed)

**Prerequisites:**
- Your user role must have the **Modify** application operation enabled for **GST Identification Number (GSTIN)**
- GSTIN must already be recorded in the system

**Steps:**
- Go to **Admin > Organization > Manage > GST Identification Number (GSTIN)**
- Click **Action > Edit** in the GSTIN grid view
- Update required details
- Click **Save**

---

## Part 2: Configuring GST Rates

### Understanding GST Rate Configuration

GST rates are linked to **HSN/SAC codes** in your Item Master. When you configure rates, you set the applicable GST percentage for different product categories.

### Method 1: Update GST Rates via Excel Import

**Best for:** Bulk updates or rate changes across multiple HSN codes

**Steps:**

**1. Navigate to Data Import Module**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Utilities > Data Import > HSN/SAC Code - GST Rates**

**2. Download Template**
- Export existing HSN master using the **EXPORT** option under Action to get the template structure

**3. Prepare Excel File**

Required columns in your Excel file:

| Column Name | Description |
|---|---|
| **HSN_SAC_CODE** | Must match HSN/SAC Code in HSN/SAC Master |
| **EFFECTIVE_FROM_DATE** | Date from which new rate applies (e.g., 01-Jan-22) |
| **GST_RATE_NAME** | Must match Tax Name defined in GST Rate Master |

**Example:**
```
HSN_SAC_CODE | EFFECTIVE_FROM_DATE | GST_RATE_NAME
6204         | 01-01-2022          | 12%
6205         | 01-01-2022          | 12%
6401         | 01-01-2022          | 12%
```

**4. Upload Excel**
- Click **Browse** and select your prepared Excel file
- Map the columns to Ginesys fields
- Click **Upload**
- System validates and updates HSN masters

**5. Verify in HSN Master**
- Go to **Inventory > Product Definition > Tax Definition > HSN/SAC Master**
- Confirm rate changes are reflected

### Method 2: Manual Update in HSN Master (For Slab-Based Rates)

**Best for:** Products with price-based tax slabs

**Steps:**

**1. Navigate to HSN/SAC Master**
- Go to **Inventory > Product Definition > Tax Definition > HSN/SAC Master**
- Search and select the HSN code to update

**2. Add Slab Definition**
- Click **Action > Manage**
- Go to **Slab Definition** section
- Click **Add** to create a new slab

**3. Define Slab Details**

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| **From Price** | Lower limit of price range |
| **To Price** | Upper limit of price range |
| **Effective From Date** | Date when slab becomes effective |
| **Tax Name** | Select the applicable GST rate (e.g., 5%, 12%, 18%) |

**Example for Footwear:**
- Slab 1: From ₹0 to ₹1000 → 12% GST
- Slab 2: From ₹1001 onwards → 18% GST

**4. Save Slab**
- Click **Save**
- ⚠️ **Important:** Once saved, slabs **cannot be modified** — ensure accuracy before saving

---

## Real-World Example: GST Rate Update (Jan 1, 2022)

### Scenario: Apparel GST Rate Change from 5% to 12%

**For Apparel (No Slab Needed):**
- Single rate applies: **12%** (regardless of price)
- Use **Excel Import method** with Effective Date: 01-Jan-22

**For Footwear (Slab-Based):**
- Below ₹1000: **12%**
- Above ₹1000: **18%**
- Use **Manual Slab Definition** in HSN Master

---

## Important Notes

⚠️ **Critical Points:**

1. **Effective From Date Extension** — If Government extends effective date, manually update HSN masters as Excel import doesn't support date modification
2. **Slab Lock** — Once slab is saved, it cannot be edited; re-check before saving
3. **Pending Orders** — If rates change mid-month:
   - Cancel pending orders
   - Re-upload with new rates via bulk import
   - Reference original Order No. for tracking
4. **GSTR-3B Compliance (July 2025)** — New regulations require GSTR-3B values to be auto-locked from GSTR-1/IFF; corrections only via GSTR-1A

---

## Summary Workflow

```
1. Create GSTIN in Admin > Organization > GSTIN Master
   ↓
2. Map GST Input/Output Ledgers to GSTIN
   ↓
3. Define/Update HSN/SAC Codes with Tax Rates
   ↓
4. Create GST Rate Master (if custom rates needed)
   ↓
5. Tag GST Rate to HSN Master via Excel or Manual Slab Definition
   ↓
6. Verify in Item Master & POS/Invoicing modules
```

---

## Reference Modules

- **Admin > Organization > Manage > GST Identification Number (GSTIN)**
- **Admin > Utilities > Data Import > HSN/SAC Code - GST Rates**
- **Inventory > Product Definition > Tax Definition > HSN/SAC Master**
- **Inventory > Product Definition > Tax Definition > GST Rate Master**
- **Admin > Finance > Ledgers** (for GST Input/Output ledgers)

---

## Video Tutorial

📺 **[How to update the GST rates for HSN in Ginesys](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6zqhfcZEAE4)** — Step-by-step visual guide

---

how to create users and assign roles.answer:
# How to Create Users and Assign Roles

This guide covers creating users in **Ginesys ERP** and assigning them appropriate roles and permissions. There are two main workflows depending on your setup.

---

## Part 1: Create Roles First (Prerequisites)

Before creating users, you must define **Roles** that specify what modules and operations each user can access.

### Step-by-Step: Add a New Role

**Prerequisites:**
- Your user role must have the **Add** app operation enabled for **Role** feature in **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > Role**

**1. Navigate to Roles Module**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > Role**
- List view of existing roles opens

**2. Click Add Button**
- The **Role** window opens

**3. Fill Role Information**

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| **Role Name** (Mandatory) | Enter a unique name for the role (e.g., "Purchase Manager", "Sales Executive", "Warehouse Officer") |
| **Description** | Optional field to describe the role's purpose |
| **Profile Name** (Mandatory) | Select from dropdown (defines the user profile type) |

**4. Select Modules to Grant Access**

- In the **Available Module** section, select the modules this role needs access to
  - Examples: **POS**, **Inventory**, **Procurement**, **Sales & Distribution**, **Reports**, **Admin**

**⚠️ Important:** If you select the **lowest level** (sub-module) of a module, all **parent modules** automatically get selected

**5. Assign App Operations**

- Click on a specific **Form** in the module
- In **Available Operation** section, select the operations:
  - **Add** — Create new records
  - **Edit** (or **Modify**) — Modify existing records
  - **Delete** — Delete records
  - **View/Print** — View and print records (for Reports)

**Example Configuration:**
```
Role: "Purchase Manager"
Modules: Procurement > Purchase Order
Operations: Add ✓, Edit ✓, Delete ✓
           Inventory > Stock > Item Master
Operations: View ✓ (Read-only)
```

**6. Save Role**
- Click **Save & Close**
- Confirmation: Role is now created and available for assignment to users

---

### Edit an Existing Role

**Prerequisites:**
- Your user role must have the **Edit** app operation enabled for **Role**

**Steps:**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > Role**
- Double-click the role you want to modify
- Edit module access and operations as needed
- Click **Save & Close**

---

### Delete a Role

**Prerequisites:**
- Your user role must have the **Delete** app operation enabled for **Role**

⚠️ **Constraint:** Roles **cannot be deleted** if they're already assigned to users. You can only **extinct** them instead.

**Steps:**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > Role**
- Select the role
- Click **Action > Delete**
- Confirmation dialog appears
- Click **Yes** to delete permanently

---

## Part 2: Create ERP Users and Assign Roles

### Step-by-Step: Add a New ERP User

**Prerequisites:**
- Your user role must have the **Add** app operation enabled for **User** feature in **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > User**
- At least one **Role** must already be created

**1. Navigate to Users Module**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > User > Users**
- List view of existing users opens

**2. Click Add Button**
- The **User** window opens with two sections: **User Information** and **Tag Site**

**3. Fill User Information Section** (All Mandatory)

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| **User No.** | Unique identifier for the user (e.g., "USR001", "MGR-PUR-01") |
| **Name** | Full name of the user |
| **Login Name** | Username for system login (auto-populated from current user; change it) |
| **Password** | User's login password |
| **Menu Role** (Mandatory) | Select the **Role** created in Part 1 (defines module & operation access) |
| **Profile** (Mandatory) | Select user profile type from dropdown |
| **Report Role** | Optional; select if user needs access to specific reports |

**⚠️ Password Requirements (if Strong Password Enforced):**
- Minimum **8 characters**
- At least **1 uppercase** [A-Z]
- At least **1 lowercase** [a-z]
- At least **1 digit** [0-9]
- At least **1 non-alphanumeric** [~!@#$%^&*_-+=`|\(){}[]:;"'<>,.?]
- ❌ **Not allowed:** Forward slash (/) and Space

**Example:**
```
User No.: USR-SAL-001
Name: Rajesh Kumar
Login Name: rajesh.kumar
Password: Rajesh@Kumar123
Menu Role: Sales Executive
Profile: Standard User
```

**4. Assign Sites (Tag Site Section)**

**⚠️ Important Site Rules:**
- ✅ You can assign **multiple sites** to a user
- ✅ At least **one Organization or Managed site** must be assigned
- ❌ Assignment of **only unmanaged sites** is **NOT allowed**
- ❌ Unmanaged sites **cannot login** to Ginesys Web
- ❌ Unmanaged sites **cannot be set as default site**

**Steps:**
- In **Available Site** section, select the sites the user can access
- Click the **right arrow (→)** button to move selected sites to **Assigned Site** section
- Set one site as **Default Site** (user logs in to this site by default)

**Example:**
```
Available Sites: HQ Store, Branch-1, Branch-2, Warehouse
Assigned Sites: HQ Store (Default) ✓, Branch-1 ✓
```

**💡 Tip:** The menu and app operations available to the user depend on the **default site assigned**. If no Organization/Managed site is default, many ERP modules will be unavailable.

**5. Optional: Ignore Lock Days Privilege**

- Check the box: **"Ignore lock days on last date of any open accounting year (for financial documents only)"**
- Allows this user to post transactions even on locked period last date
- Only applicable for financial documents

**6. Save User**
- Click **Save & Close**
- Confirmation: "User created successfully"
- User can now login with Login Name and Password

---

### Edit an Existing User

**Prerequisites:**
- Your user role must have the **Modify** app operation enabled for **User**

**Steps:**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > User > Users**
- Double-click the user you want to edit
- **User No.** field cannot be modified (read-only)
- Edit other fields as needed:
  - Change **Password**
  - Update **Menu Role** (change permissions)
  - Add/Remove **Sites**
  - Update **Report Role**
- Click **Save & Close**

---

### Delete a User

**Prerequisites:**
- Your user role must have the **Delete** app operation enabled for **User**

⚠️ **Constraint:** If any referential records exist with the user, you **cannot delete** that user. You can only **extinct** them instead.

**Steps:**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > User > Users**
- Select the user
- Click **Action > Delete**
- Confirmation dialog: "Are you sure you want to delete current Record?"
- Click **Yes** to delete permanently
- Message: "Record deleted successfully"

---

## Part 3: Bulk Create Users (Alternative Method)

For creating multiple users at once, use **Bulk Import**.

### Step-by-Step: Bulk Add Users

**1. Navigate to Bulk Import**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > User > Users**
- Click **Bulk Import** button (if available in your version)

**2. Download Template**
- Click **Click here** link to download the CSV template
- Template file downloads automatically

**3. Prepare CSV File**

Required columns:

| Column | Example | Mandatory |
|---|---|---|
| User No. | USR-001 | Yes |
| Name | Rajesh Kumar | Yes |
| Login Name | rajesh.kumar | Yes |
| Password | Rajesh@Kumar123 | Yes |
| Menu Role | Sales Executive | Yes |
| Profile | Standard User | Yes |
| Sites | HQ Store; Branch-1 | Yes |
| Default Site | HQ Store | Yes |

**4. Upload CSV**
- Click **Browse** to select your filled CSV file
- Or **drag & drop** the file
- Click **Upload**

**5. Review Results**
- If errors exist, view invalid entries
- Correct the errors and retry upload
- Success notification appears when complete
- Refresh page to see new users

---

## Part 4: Grant Role Privileges (Fine-Tune Permissions)

After assigning a role to a user, you can customize specific module-level permissions.

### Step-by-Step: Configure Role Privileges

**1. Navigate to Role Privileges**
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Admin > Security > Role Privileges**

**2. Select a Role**
- Click **Browse** button under **Criteria**
- **Select Role Name** dialog opens
- Choose the role (e.g., "Purchase Manager")
- Click **OK**
- Tree structure of modules appears on right side

**3. Grant Permissions**

**To Assign Rights:**
- Right-click on a **module name** in the tree
- A permission level box appears
- Select appropriate **access level**:
  - **Full Access**
  - **Read Only**
  - **Restricted**
  - **No Access**

**⚠️ Important:** Assigning rights to a **parent module** automatically assigns rights to **all sub-modules** within it.

**Example:**
```
Module: Sales & Distribution
├── Sales Order (Full Access)
├── Delivery Challan (Read Only)
└── Sales Invoice (Full Access)
```

**4. Modify Sub-Module Rights**

- Click on a **specific sub-module** (e.g., "Delivery Challan")
- Right-click and select desired privilege level
- This overrides parent module settings for that specific module

**To Remove Rights:**
- Double-click the checkbox beside the module name
- Removes access completely

**5. Save Changes**
- Click **Save** button
- Permissions are now applied to all users with this role

---

## Complete Workflow Example

### Scenario: Create Sales Executive with POS Access

**Step 1: Create Role "Sales Executive"**
```
Role Name: Sales Executive
Modules: 
  ✓ POS
  ✓ Sales & Distribution
  ✓ Reports
  
Operations:
  POS > Add ✓, Edit ✓, Delete ✓
  Sales & Distribution > Sales Invoice > View ✓ (Read-only)
  Reports > All Reports > View ✓
```

**Step 2: Create User & Assign Role**
```
User No.: USR-SAL-001
Name: Ananya Sharma
Login Name: ananya.sharma
Password: Ananya@Sharma123
Menu Role: Sales Executive
Sites: Branch-1 (Default), HQ Store
```

**Step 3: User Login**
- User logs in with: **ananya.sharma** / **Ananya@Sharma123**
- Default site: **Branch-1**
- Accessible modules: **POS**, **Sales & Distribution**, **Reports**
- Permissions: Can create/edit POS, view invoices, run reports

---

## Troubleshooting

### User Gets "User not authorised to login. No privileges assigned."

**Cause:** User has no role assigned or role has no module access

**Solution:**
1. Go to **Admin > Security > User > Users**
2. Edit the user
3. Assign a **Menu Role**
4. Go to **Admin > Security > Role Privileges**
5. Select the role and grant module access
6. Save and retry login

### User Cannot Access Specific Module

**Cause:** Role not configured for that module

**Solution:**
1. Go to **Admin > Security > Role**
2. Edit the user's role
3. Add the module to **Available Module** section
4. Select **Add/Edit/Delete** operations as needed
5. Click **Save & Close**
6. User can now access module

---

## Quick Reference: User & Role Permissions Matrix

| Operation | Add Role | Edit Role | Delete Role | Add User | Edit User | Delete User | Assign Role |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Admin User** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Manager (with Role Mgmt)** | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |
| **Standard User** | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |

---

how to view the stock point transfer reg.answer:
# How to View the Stock Point Transfer Register

The **Stock Point Transfer Register** is a report that provides a comprehensive view of all stock transfers between different stock points in your system. This register helps you track stock movements, monitor transfer activities, and maintain proper inventory records.

## Prerequisites

- Your user role must have access to **Reports** module with appropriate viewing permissions
- Stock Point Transfers must exist in the system to generate meaningful reports
- Ensure you have access to the **Back Office** reporting section

## Step-by-Step Guide

### 1. Navigate to Reports Module
- Go to **Ginesys POS > Back Office > Reports**
- Look for **Stock Transfer** or **Inventory Reports** section

### 2. Access Stock Point Transfer Register
- Select **Stock Point Transfer Register** from the reports list
- The report parameter window opens

### 3. Set Report Parameters

Configure the following filters to view specific transfer data:

#### Date Range
- **From Date** — Select the starting date for the report
- **To Date** — Select the ending date for the report

#### Stock Point Filters
- **Transfer From Stock Point** — Select the source stock point (optional)
- **Transfer To Stock Point** — Select the destination stock point (optional)
- **All Stock Points** — Check this to include all stock points

#### Additional Filters
- **Document Status** — Choose from:
  - **Open** — Unposted transfers
  - **Closed** — Posted transfers
  - **All** — Both open and closed transfers
- **Item Category** — Filter by specific item categories (optional)
- **Document Number Range** — Specify transfer document numbers (optional)

### 4. Generate Report
- Click **Generate** or **View Report** button
- The Stock Point Transfer Register displays with the following information:
  - **Transfer Date**
  - **Document Number**
  - **From Stock Point**
  - **To Stock Point**
  - **Item Details** (Code, Description, Barcode)
  - **Quantity Transferred**
  - **Transfer Status**
  - **User/Operator Details**

### 5. Report Actions

Once the report is generated, you can:

#### Print Report
- Click the **Print** icon in the report viewer
- Select your printer and print settings
- Click **Print**

#### Export Report
- Use **Export** options to save as:
  - **Excel** format
  - **PDF** format
  - **CSV** format

#### Filter Results
- Use the search functionality within the report
- Apply additional filters if available

## Report Information Details

The **Stock Point Transfer Register** typically includes:

- **Transfer Summary** — Total number of transfers and quantities
- **Item-wise Details** — Complete breakdown of transferred items
- **Stock Point Analysis** — Transfer patterns between different points
- **Status Information** — Posted vs. unposted transfers
- **Date-wise Grouping** — Transfers organized by date ranges
- **User Tracking** — Who performed each transfer

## Additional Tips

💡 **Best Practices**:
- Run the report regularly to monitor stock movement patterns
- Use date filters to focus on specific periods
- Export data for further analysis in Excel
- Compare with physical stock counts for accuracy

⚠️ **Important Notes**:
- Only completed and saved transfers appear in the register
- Open (unposted) transfers may show different status
- Ensure transfers are properly posted to reflect in inventory

---

## Sources

📖 [How To: Manage a Stock Points Transfer at a Store](https://ginesysone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/GPOS/pages/29200920)

inventory in ginesys.answer:
# Inventory Management in Ginesys

Ginesys provides a comprehensive **Inventory Management System** that enables efficient tracking, control, and management of stock across your retail operations. The inventory module is designed to handle all aspects from product definition to warehouse management.

## Core Inventory Components

### 1. Product Definition & Master Data
The foundation of inventory management starts with proper product setup:

- **Item Hierarchy Management** — Organize products by categories, sub-categories, and classifications
- **Inventory Setup** — Configure basic inventory parameters and controls  
- **Tax Definition** — Set up GST and other tax structures for inventory items
- **Stock Point Management** — Define and manage different storage locations

### 2. Inventory Operations
Ginesys offers extensive operational capabilities for inventory management:

#### **Requisitions**
- Record demand/requirements from various stock locations
- Process requisition requests sent by stock points
- Track requisition status and fulfillment

#### **Stock Audits & Adjustments**
- Perform **Inventory Audits** to verify actual vs. system stock
- Handle **Miscellaneous Entries** for stock corrections
- Adjust inventory discrepancies between physical and system quantities
- Maintain audit trails for all adjustments

#### **Product Conversions**
The **Conversion Module** allows transformation of products:
- **Split Operations** — Create new items by modifying existing item properties
  - Generate new barcodes and RSP (Retail Sale Price)
  - Useful when repricing items due to market conditions
- Change product forms to more saleable variants

#### **Stock Transfers**
- Document and manage **Stock Point Transfers** between locations
- Track inter-location stock movements
- Maintain transfer documentation and approvals

### 3. Warehouse Management System (WMS)

Ginesys includes a built-in **WMS** for advanced warehouse operations:

#### **Setup Components**
- **Bin Master Setup** — Define storage locations within warehouses
- Configure bin hierarchies and storage zones

#### **Transaction Management**
- **Put-away Operations** — Store incoming stock in designated bins
- **Pick Operations** — Retrieve stock from bins for orders
- **Stock Adjustments** — Handle bin-level inventory corrections
- **Stock Reservations** — Reserve inventory for specific orders
- **Pick List Management** — Create and confirm picking lists

## Key Features & Benefits

### **Centralized Control**
- Single source of truth for all inventory data
- Reduces human errors through standardized processes
- Ensures smooth data flow across modules

### **Real-time Tracking**
- Live inventory updates across all locations
- Immediate visibility of stock levels and movements
- Automated alerts for low stock situations

### **Multi-location Management**
- Handle inventory across multiple stores and warehouses
- Inter-branch transfer capabilities
- Location-specific stock reports

### **Comprehensive Reporting**
- Stock movement reports
- Inventory valuation reports  
- Audit trail documentation
- Transfer registers and summaries

## Getting Started with Inventory

### **Step 1: Master Data Setup**
1. Configure **Item Hierarchy**
2. Set up **Stock Points** for your locations
3. Define **Tax Structures**
4. Create **Product Masters**

### **Step 2: Operational Setup**  
1. Configure **Document Numbering Schemes**
2. Set up **User Roles** and permissions
3. Define **Approval Workflows**
4. Configure **Report Templates**

### **Step 3: Daily Operations**
1. Process **Requisitions** from stores
2. Perform regular **Stock Audits**
3. Handle **Stock Transfers** between locations
4. Manage **Conversions** and adjustments

## Best Practices

✅ **Regular Audits** — Conduct periodic stock audits to maintain accuracy
✅ **Proper Documentation** — Maintain complete records of all transactions
✅ **Role-based Access** — Assign appropriate permissions to users
✅ **Training** — Ensure staff understand inventory processes
✅ **System Integration** — Connect inventory with POS and accounting modules

## Advanced Capabilities

- **Batch/Serial Number Tracking** — For items requiring detailed traceability
- **Expiry Date Management** — Handle perishable goods effectively  
- **Multi-unit Inventory** — Manage items in different units of measure
- **Supplier Integration** — Link with purchase orders and vendor management

---

how to set a process in production modul.answer:
# How to Set a Process in Production Module of Ginesys

A **Process** in the Production module defines the manufacturing steps required to transform raw materials into finished goods. Processes are essential components of the **Bill of Materials (BoM)** and must be created before setting up production workflows.

## Prerequisites

- Your user role must have access to the **Process** feature with **Add** app operation enabled in **Admin > Security > User > Role** through **Production > Setup > Manage**
- **Route Groups** must be created and configured
- Manufacturing workflow should be planned and documented

## Step-by-Step Guide

### 1. Navigate to Process Setup
- Go to **Ginesys Web > Production > Setup > Manage > Process**
- The **Process** module opens showing existing processes

### 2. Create New Process
- Click the **Add** button
- The **Add Process** window opens

### 3. Configure Process Details

#### **Basic Information**
- **Process Code** — Enter a unique identifier for the process
- **Process Name** — Provide a descriptive name for the manufacturing step
- **Description** — Add detailed description of what this process involves

#### **Route Association**
- **Route Group** — Select the route group this process belongs to
- **Operation Sequence** — Define the order of this process in the production sequence
- Only processes belonging to the selected route will be available for BoM creation

#### **Process Parameters**
- **Standard Time** — Set the expected time required for this process
- **Setup Time** — Define preparation time needed before the process begins
- **Batch Size** — Specify the optimal quantity for processing
- **Resource Requirements** — Define machinery, labor, or other resources needed

### 4. Define Process Components

#### **Input Materials**
- Specify raw materials or semi-finished goods required for this process
- Set required quantities and specifications
- Link to inventory items in the system

#### **Output Products**
- Define what products or semi-finished goods this process produces
- Set output quantities and quality specifications

#### **Quality Parameters**
- Define quality checkpoints and standards
- Set inspection requirements
- Specify acceptable tolerance levels

### 5. Configure Process Workflow

#### **Dependencies**
- Set prerequisite processes that must complete before this process begins
- Define parallel processes that can run simultaneously
- Establish critical path relationships

#### **Resource Allocation**
- Assign specific work centers or machines
- Allocate required personnel or skill sets
- Set capacity constraints

### 6. Save Process Configuration
- Review all entered information
- Click **Save** to create the process
- Confirmation message appears: *"Process created successfully"*

## Using Processes in Bill of Materials

Once processes are created, they integrate with BoM creation:

### **Process Selection in BoM**
- When creating a **Bill of Materials**, processes belonging to the selected route are displayed
- **Operation sequence** auto-populates based on process definition
- Multiple processes can be selected for complex manufacturing workflows

### **Component Assignment**
- For each process, assign required **Component Items** (raw materials)
- At least one component must be specified for each selected process
- **Assembly Items** cannot be selected as components

### **Process Integration**
- Processes link **Route Groups** with specific manufacturing steps
- Enable accurate costing and time estimation
- Support capacity planning and resource scheduling

## Process Management Features

### **Process Modification**
- Edit existing processes to update parameters
- Modify resource requirements as operations evolve
- Update time standards based on actual performance

### **Process Validation**
- System validates process sequences and dependencies
- Ensures logical flow of manufacturing operations
- Prevents conflicts in resource allocation

### **Process Reporting**
- Track process efficiency and performance
- Monitor actual vs. standard times
- Generate process utilization reports

## Best Practices

✅ **Clear Naming** — Use descriptive process names that clearly identify the operation
✅ **Accurate Timing** — Set realistic standard and setup times based on actual data
✅ **Resource Planning** — Properly define resource requirements to avoid bottlenecks
✅ **Quality Integration** — Include quality checkpoints in process definition
✅ **Regular Review** — Update processes based on continuous improvement initiatives

## Advanced Features

- **Multi-level Processes** — Create complex processes with sub-operations
- **Alternative Processes** — Define backup processes for flexibility
- **Process Costing** — Integrate with cost accounting for accurate product costing
- **Capacity Planning** — Link processes to production planning and scheduling

---

I don't have access to video tutorials for POS Orders, but I can provide you with a comprehensive step-by-step guide on how to create POS Orders in Ginesys.

# How to Create a POS Order in Ginesys

A **POS Order** is created to record customer orders, typically for customized items or items not immediately available for sale. Here's the complete process:

## Prerequisites

Your user role must have access to these features in **POS Admin > Security > Roles > Access Rights > Normal POS**:
- **POS Order**
- **Allow to Add Customer**
- **Allow to Modify Customer**
- **Deposit**
- **Reprint** (optional)

Additional requirements:
- **Document Numbering Scheme** for POS Order must exist
- **Customer Tagging** is mandatory
- **Item selection** is mandatory
- **Credit Note** as a Mode of Payment must be assigned to the POS

## Step-by-Step Process

### 1. Access POS Order
- Go to **Tasks** (or press shortcut key **F5**)
- Click **Take Order**
- The **POS Order** window opens

### 2. Tag Customer
- In the **Customer** section, click the **Browse** button
- Select an existing customer or create a new one
- Click **OK** to confirm selection

### 3. Set Due Date
- Go to **Due Date** option in the top right corner
- Enter the due date for the entire order
- *Alternative*: Enter due dates for individual items in the **Due Date** column of the Order Items list

### 4. Add Items to Order
- Click the **Select Item** tab
- The **Item Scanning** window appears
- **Scan the Barcode** or enter it manually
- Provide the **Quantity** required
- Click **Accept**
- The item gets added to the **Order Items** list
- Repeat for additional items

### 5. Assign Sales Person
- Go to the **Sales Person Name** column
- Select a sales person for each item
- *Alternative*: Click **Assign Sales Person to All** button to assign one sales person to all items

### 6. Configure Home Delivery (if needed)
- Check the **Home Delivery Required** checkbox if applicable
- Add any special delivery instructions

### 7. Save the Order
- Click **Save** button
- The system saves the POS Order

### 8. Handle Deposit
- After saving, a dialog box appears asking: *"Do you want to take a deposit from the customer?"*
- Click **Yes** if you want to collect a deposit, or **No** to skip

### 9. Process Deposit (if taking deposit)
- If you clicked **Yes**, the **Deposit/Adjustment** window appears
- Go to **Mode of Payment** list and select the payment method
- Enter the deposit amount in the **Amount** column
- Click **Issue Credit Note and Save**
- Click **OK** in the Record Save window
- A **Credit Note** for the deposit amount is created

## Important Notes

💡 **Credit Note Validity**: Credit Notes are valid for the number of days specified by HO through Master Policy. Check this at **Admin > Policy > Master Policy > User Settings** under "**Validity of Credit Notes (issued against other bills) in days**"

💡 **Alternative Access**: POS Orders can also be created from **POS Back Office > Transactions > POS Order** following the same process

⚠️ **Mandatory Fields**: Customer tagging and item selection are required to complete a POS Order

## What Happens After Order Creation

1. **Order Tracking** — The system assigns a unique POS Order number
2. **Customer Notification** — Customer receives order confirmation details  
3. **Inventory Allocation** — Items may be reserved based on your configuration
4. **Credit Note** — If deposit taken, credit note is available for future use
5. **Order Fulfillment** — Order can be converted to sale when items arrive

---

For video tutorials, I recommend checking:
- **Ginesys Training Portal** (if you have access)
- **Ginesys YouTube Channel** 
- **Contact your Ginesys support team** for training materials

how to update inventory after creating a.answer:
# How to Update Inventory After Creating a GRC

When you create a **Goods Receive Challan (GRC)** in Ginesys, the inventory is **automatically updated** through the **Release** process. Here's how inventory gets updated and the steps involved:

---

## Automatic Inventory Update Process

### 1. GRC Creation Impact
- **GRC affects Inventory** but has **no impact on Finance**
- Upon creation, GRC is in **Draft/Open** status
- **No inventory movement occurs** until the GRC is **Released**

### 2. Release Process Updates Inventory

#### **For Organization Sites:**
- GRC is **automatically released** upon creation
- **Inventory updates immediately** without manual intervention
- Stock quantities increase automatically at the receiving stock point

#### **For Managed Sites:**
- GRC must be **manually released** to update inventory
- Follow the release steps below to update inventory

---

## Step-by-Step Guide to Release GRC and Update Inventory

### Prerequisites
- User role must have access to the **Release** app operation in **Receipt** feature (Procurement - Operation - Goods In/Out)
- **GRC must be created** and in **Open/Draft** status
- GRC must **not be already Released**

### Release Steps

1. **Navigate to GRC Module**
   - Go to **Ginesys Web > Procurement > Operations > Goods In/Out > Receipt**
   - **Goods Receive Challan** module opens with list of created GRCs

2. **Select GRC to Release**
   - Select **one or more GRC** that you want to release
   - Use **Filter** to find specific GRCs if needed (default shows last 7 days)

3. **Release the GRC**
   - Go to **Action > Release**
   - Confirmation message appears: *"Document(s) once released cannot be modified. Do you want to proceed?"*
   - Click **Yes** to proceed

4. **Inventory Update Confirmation**
   - Message appears: *"Receipt No: <GRC No.> Released Successfully"*
   - **Inventory is now updated** with received quantities
   - Stock levels increase at the designated **Stock Point**

---

## What Happens to Inventory During Release

### Stock Movement Details:
- **Stock Quantities Increase** at the receiving stock point specified in GRC
- **Item-wise stock levels** update in real-time
- **Batch/Serial information** (if applicable) gets recorded in inventory
- **Stock Point-wise stock reports** reflect the new quantities
- **Last Receipt Date** updates for each item

### Inventory Impact:
- **Available Stock** increases by received quantities
- **Stock Valuation** updates based on GRC rates
- **Item Movement History** records the GRC transaction
- **Batch/Serial Tracking** (if enabled) maintains traceability

---

## Verification of Inventory Update

### Check Stock Levels:
1. **Go to Inventory Reports**
   - Navigate to **Reports > Inventory > Stock Reports**
   - Run **Stock Point wise Stock Report**
   - Verify quantities match GRC received quantities

2. **View Item Movement**
   - Check **Item Movement Reports** for transaction history
   - GRC transaction appears with **Receipt** transaction type

3. **Stock Point Verification**
   - Go to **Sales & Distribution > Delivery > Delivery Challan**
   - Check **Stock Point wise stock** for items
   - Quantities should reflect GRC additions

---

## Important Notes About Inventory Updates

⚠️ **Key Points:**

- **Organization Sites**: Inventory updates **automatically** on GRC creation
- **Managed Sites**: Manual **Release** required for inventory update
- **Cannot modify released GRC**: Once released, GRC cannot be edited
- **Stock Posting**: Release creates stock postings at store level
- **Real-time Update**: Inventory reflects immediately upon release

### **Revert Process** (If Needed):
- If you need to **undo inventory update**, use **Action > Revert**
- Revert operation **removes stock postings** and returns GRC to draft status
- Confirmation: *"Selected documents will not be un-posted, and stock postings done at store will also be reverted. Do you want to proceed?"*

---

## Troubleshooting Inventory Issues

### If Inventory Doesn't Update:

1. **Check GRC Status**
   - Verify GRC shows **Released** status in list view
   - If still **Open**, perform Release operation

2. **Verify User Permissions**
   - Ensure user has **Release** app operation access
   - Check role assignments in **Admin > Security > Roles**

3. **Check Stock Point**
   - Verify correct **Stock Point** was selected in GRC
   - Check stock levels in the correct stock point location

4. **Site Type Verification**
   - **Organization Sites**: Should auto-release and auto-update inventory
   - **Managed Sites**: Require manual release

---

## Summary

🔄 **Inventory Update Flow:**
1. **Create GRC** → Inventory not affected
2. **Release GRC** → Inventory automatically updates
3. **Verify Stock** → Check reports to confirm update

✅ **Result**: Stock quantities increase at receiving stock point, inventory valuation updates, and item movement history records the transaction.

---

📖 [How To: Create Goods Receive Challan against GRC Advice at POS Back Office](https://ginesysone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/GPOS/pages/29200659)

# Quick Void & Recreate Delivery Slip - Video Tutorial

I have a specific video tutorial that covers the **Quick Void & Recreate Delivery Slip** feature in Ginesys POS.

## Video Tutorial

📺 **"Feature Update - Quick void & recreate Delivery Slip provision added"**

🔗 **Watch Here**: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xfI4V-s8dQ0

This video demonstrates the new quick void and recreate functionality for delivery slips, which allows you to:

- **Quickly void** an existing delivery slip with incorrect entries
- **Instantly recreate** a new delivery slip with corrected information
- **Streamline the correction process** without manual re-entry of all items

---

## What You'll Learn

The video covers:

✅ **Quick Void Process** - How to void delivery slips efficiently
✅ **Recreation Steps** - Automatically recreating delivery slips with corrections
✅ **Time-Saving Features** - Avoiding manual re-entry of items
✅ **Workflow Improvements** - Enhanced delivery slip management

---

## Prerequisites for Quick Void & Recreate

Before using this feature, ensure:

- Your user role has access to the **Void** feature in **Admin > Security > Role > Access Rights > POS**
- **Allow Cancellation of Held Delivery** permission enabled
- The delivery slip to be voided exists in the system
- Proper void reasons are configured at HO level (if mandatory)

---

## Additional Related Videos

For comprehensive delivery slip management, also check out:

📺 **"GINESYS Tutorials: How to complete the POS order by making delivery"**
🔗 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xVES4e-Ni5U

📺 **"GINESYS Tutorials: How to give discounts in GINESYS POS"**
🔗 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FeCn7umneM4

---

## Standard Void Process (Manual Method)

For reference, the traditional void process involves:

1. **Open Delivery Slip** module in GINESYS POS
2. **Click Slip** (or press **F4**) → **Void**
3. **Search** for the delivery slip to void
4. **Select** the required slip and click **Void**

The **Quick Void & Recreate** feature automates and streamlines this entire process!

how to generate pos order youtube video.answer:
Here are the YouTube videos specifically for creating POS Orders in Ginesys:

## POS Order Training Videos

**1. How to Create a POS Order**
- **Title:** "GINESYS Tutorials: How to create a POS Order"
- **Link:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FOCaJ0qUyG0
- Complete step-by-step tutorial on creating POS orders from scratch

**2. Understanding POS Order Elements**
- **Title:** "GINESYS Tutorials: POS Order and its elements"
- **Link:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ez0Z7rv7aew
- Learn about all components and features of POS orders

**3. Completing POS Orders with Delivery**
- **Title:** "GINESYS Tutorials: How to complete the POS order by making delivery"
- **Link:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xVES4e-Ni5U
- Tutorial on fulfilling and delivering POS orders to customers

**4. Canceling POS Orders**
- **Title:** "GINESYS Tutorials: How to cancel a POS order"
- **Link:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j8DHFnL4KMc
- Learn the proper process for canceling POS orders when needed

## What You'll Learn

- **Complete Order Process:** From creation to delivery
- **Customer Management:** How to tag customers and handle deposits
- **Item Selection:** Scanning and selecting products for orders
- **Order Fulfillment:** Delivery processes and completion steps
- **Order Management:** Handling cancellations and modifications

These videos provide comprehensive visual guidance for managing the entire POS order lifecycle in Ginesys.
